content.question: A rectangle has a length of 10 meters and a width of 5 meters. If both dimensions are doubled, what is the new area?
content.model_output: The original area is 10 × 5 = 50 square meters. If both dimensions are doubled, the new length is 20 and the new width is 10. The new area is 20 × 10 = 200 square meters. However, since both dimensions were doubled, the area should also double, so the new area is 100 square meters.
content.break_point: Replacement of the correct computed value (200) with an unstated linear-scaling assumption
content.TDelta: At transition from exact computation (200) to flawed scaling rule
content.Omega_summary: Stable during exact geometric computation -> sharp decay at scaling override
content.SEI: High on correct structure (200), followed by false saturation under incorrect scaling assumption
